title: Roblox Q3 2023 Earnings Preview: Analysts Expect Revenue Growth, But Losses to Continue
text: Analysts expected Roblox to report Q3 2023 revenue of $820.08 million, representing 16.9% year-over-year growth. The consensus EPS estimate was negative $0.51, a 2.0% year-over-year improvement, indicating analysts expected the company to continue posting losses. Analysts forecasted several operational metrics: Bookings were forecast to reach $1.70 billion, compared to $1.13 billion in the year-ago quarter. Daily Active Users (DAUs) were predicted to reach 122.46 million, compared to 88.90 million year-ago. Total Hours Engaged were expected to come in at 29.49 billion, versus 20.70 billion in the prior year. Raymond James analyst Andrew Marok noted that Roblox's long-term expansion could be driven by strategic efforts to capture more of the video game entertainment market and attract an older demographic, with advertising growth still in early stages and potential expansion into non-gaming areas like education.
